openmeter/billing/validators.purpose: Organisational package grouping cross-domain billing guard validators. Its two children register pre/post-mutation hooks into customer.Service and subscription.Service respectively, enforcing billing pre-conditions without introducing circular imports between the billing, customer, and subscription packages.

patterns.name: Registration at wiring time, not compile time
patterns.description: Each validator is constructed in app/common and registered via customerService.RegisterRequestValidator() or subscriptionService.RegisterHook(). Neither child imports the other domain's package at the call site — the coupling is in the DI layer only.
patterns.example: app/common wires NewValidator(...) then calls customerService.RegisterRequestValidator(v) or subscriptionService.RegisterHook(v)

patterns.name: Embed noop base structs
patterns.description: Validators embed customer.NoopRequestValidator or subscription.NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook so they only override the specific lifecycle methods they need. Unused hook methods are left as noops.
patterns.example: type Validator struct { customer.NoopRequestValidator; ... } or type Validator struct { subscription.NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook; ... }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: customer/customer.go uses `var _ customer.RequestValidator = (*Validator)(nil)` to assert the interface at compile time. Add this to every new validator.

patterns.name: Nil-guard constructors returning error
patterns.description: Both NewValidator functions check each injected service for nil and return an error. This catches misconfigured wiring at startup.
patterns.example: if billingService == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("billing service is required") }

patterns.name: models.NewGenericConflictError for billing setup failures
patterns.description: subscription/validator.go wraps validation errors in models.NewGenericConflictError so the HTTP layer renders a 409. Use this — not a plain error — for billing pre-condition failures.

patterns.name: Sync-before-validate in customer deletion
patterns.description: customer/customer.go calls syncService.SynchronizeSubscription before listing invoices so pending subscription charges are reflected in the invoice state before the gate check runs.

patterns.name: errors.Join for multi-entity validation errors
patterns.description: customer/customer.go collects per-invoice errors into a slice and returns errors.Join(errs...) so the caller sees all blocking invoices at once, not just the first.
patterns.example: errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("invoice %s is not in final state...", id)); return errors.Join(errs...)

key_file_guides.role: Implements customer.RequestValidator. ValidateDeleteCustomer syncs pending subscriptions then blocks deletion when non-final standard invoices or active gathering invoices exist.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Skipping the SynchronizeSubscription call before ListInvoices will cause false-negative gate checks; the subscriptionService field is intentionally nullable (no nil guard) — do not add nil guard without checking app/common wiring.

key_file_guides.role: Implements subscription.SubscriptionCommandHook via AfterCreate/AfterUpdate. Validates that the customer's installed billing app supports tax calculation, invoicing, and payment collection before a subscription with priced ratecards becomes active.
key_file_guides.watch_for: hasBillableItems short-circuits validation for free subscriptions — ensure this exit is still correct for any new ratecard types; capability slice is hardcoded to three app.CapabilityType values, not sourced from the app.

anti_patterns: Adding ValidateCreateCustomer or ValidateUpdateCustomer in the customer validator without a billing-domain reason — general lifecycle belongs in the customer package.
Calling billing.Adapter or Ent directly from either validator — all reads must go through billing.Service.
Returning a plain error instead of models.NewGenericConflictError for billing setup failures in the subscription validator — the HTTP layer maps GenericConflictError to 409.
Adding app-type-specific branching (e.g., 'if Stripe do X') in the subscription validator — capability validation is intentionally app-agnostic.
Using context.Background() instead of propagating the incoming ctx parameter.

decisions.decision: Validators live under billing/validators/ rather than inside customer/ or subscription/
decisions.rationale: Billing has dependency edges to both customer and subscription; placing validators here avoids import cycles by keeping the billing-domain logic in billing's own package tree.

decisions.decision: Subscription sync is driven inside the customer deletion validator rather than as a pre-hook in the subscription service
decisions.rationale: The sync ensures invoice state is up-to-date at the moment of deletion validation. Doing it inside the billing validator keeps the customer package free of billing concerns.

decisions.decision: Post-create/post-update (AfterCreate/AfterUpdate) hooks for subscription validation rather than pre-create
decisions.rationale: Billing setup validation checks state that only exists after the subscription is persisted (e.g. customer override profile, app assignment); a pre-create check would race against incomplete state.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new billing validator for a domain entity (pattern: embed noop, nil-guard constructor, compile-time assertion, wrap errors in GenericConflictError)
